results.description: ANATOMY AND FUNCTION OF A FUNGIFORM SPECIFIC MECHANORECEPTOR - PROJECT SUMMARY/ABSTRACT SOMATOSENSORY INNERVATION OF THE ORAL CAVITY ENABLES US TO DETECT THE VARIOUS TEXTURES, TEMPERATURES, AND CHEMICALS OF THE FOODS WE CONSUME. THE COMBINATION OF THESE FEATURES ELICITS THE PERCEPTION CALLED “MOUTHFEEL”, WHICH PLAYS AN ESSENTIAL ROLE IN FOOD CHOICE. THE NEURON SUBTYPES WHICH PLAY A ROLE IN THIS PERCEPTION HAVE NOT BEEN IDENTIFIED. I AM SPECULATING THAT THE TONGUE IS INNERVATED BY SUBTYPES OF MECHANICALLY SENSITIVE NEURONS, EACH HAVING A UNIQUE SENSITIVITY, MORPHOLOGY, RECEPTIVE FIELD SIZE, AND ADAPTATION PROPERTIES. HERE I WILL USE A GENETIC LABEL TO FULLY CHARACTERIZE ONE SUCH NEURON SUBTYPE INNERVATING THE TONGUE. THIS GENETIC LABEL CAN THEN BE USED TO GAIN A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN GENETICS, ANATOMY, AND FUNCTION OF ORAL SENSORY NEURONS MEDIATING TOUCH. THE GOALS OF THIS PROPOSAL ARE: 1) TO DETERMINE IF PARVALBUMIN (PVALB+) IS A GENETIC LABEL FOR A MECHANOSENSORY SUBTYPE; 2) TO FURTHER CHARACTERIZE PVALB+ NEURONS BY DETERMINING IF THEY ARE MYELINATED, AND IF THEY OVERLAP WITH OTHER LABELS; 3) TO DETERMINE THE RECEPTIVE FIELD SIZE AND ENDING MORPHOLOGIES OF PVALB+ NEURONS; AND 4) TO DETERMINE THE FUNCTIONAL CHARACTERISTICS OF PVALB+ NEURONS. TO ACCOMPLISH THIS, I WILL USE A COMBINATION OF IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY, SINGLE CELL ELECTROPHYSIOLOGY, AND CALCIUM IMAGING. TOGETHER THESE EXPERIMENTS WILL TEST THE HYPOTHESIS THAT PVALB+ NEURONS IDENTIFY A MYELINATED, RAPIDLY ADAPTING, FAST-CONDUCTING SUBTYPE OF TOUCH NEURON THAT PRIMARILY INNERVATES SINGLE FUNGIFORM PAPILLAE WITH BASKET LIKE ENDINGS. IF SO, THESE NEURONS WOULD BE GOOD AT INDICATING THE LOCATION OF A FOOD ON THE TONGUE SURFACE. THIS IS THE FIRST STEP TOWARD FUTURE STUDIES DESIGNED TO EXAMINE THE CENTRAL CIRCUITY AND BEHAVIORAL CONTRIBUTIONS OF PVALB+ NEURONS INNERVATING THE ORAL CAVITY.
